briefdescription.para: Repeat the symbol on the jump.
Append a sequence of DONT_CAREs to the symbol on the jump.
A non-deterministic finite transducer.

memberdef.type: std::is_same< bool_pack< true, Ts::value... >, bool_pack< Ts::value..., true > >
memberdef.name: conjunction

briefdescription.para: Check that for all values in a pack Ts are 'true'.
Check that all types in a sequence of parameters Ts are of type T.
Map of additional parameter name and value pairs.
An epsilon symbol which is now defined as the maximal value of data type used for symbols.
Compute non-deterministic union.

detaileddescription.para:
Does not add epsilon transitions, just unites initial and final states.
Non-deterministic union of lhs and rhs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, ProductFinalStateCondition final_condition, Symbol first_epsilon, std::unordered_map< std::pair< State, State >, State > *prod_map)=delete
memberdef.name: product

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, std::unordered_map< std::pair< State, State >, State > *prod_map=nullptr, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol, State lhs_first_aux_state=Limits::max_state, State rhs_first_aux_state=Limits::max_state)
memberdef.name: intersection

briefdescription.para: Compute intersection of two NFTs.

detaileddescription.para: Both automata can contain ε-transitions. Epsilons will be handled as alphabet symbols.
Automata must share alphabets. //TODO: this is not implemented yet. Transducers must have equal values of num_of_levels.

parameterdescription.para: First NFT to compute intersection for.
Second NFT to compute intersection for.
Mapping of pairs of the original states (lhs_state, rhs_state) to new product states (not used internally, allocated only when !=nullptr, expensive).

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE.
The first auxiliary state in lhs. Two auxiliary states does not form a product state.
The first auxiliary state in rhs. Two auxiliary states does not form a product state.

simplesect.para: NFT as a product of NFTs lhs and rhs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, const utils::OrdVector< Level > &lhs_sync_levels, const utils::OrdVector< Level > &rhs_sync_levels, bool project_out_sync_levels=true, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol)
memberdef.name: compose

briefdescription.para: Composes two NFTs (lhs || rhs; read as "rhs after lhs").

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the composition of two NFTs, lhs and rhs, by aligning their synchronization levels. Transitions between two synchronization levels are ordered as follows: first the transitions of lhs, then the transitions of rhs followed by next synchronization level (if exists). By default, synchronization levels are projected out from the resulting NFT.
Vectors of synchronization levels have to be non-empty and of the the same size.

parameterdescription.para: First transducer to compose.
Second transducer to compose.
Ordered vector of synchronization levels of the lhs.
Ordered vector of synchronization levels of the rhs.
Whether we want to project out the synchronization levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE.

simplesect.para: A new NFT after the composition.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, Level lhs_sync_level=1, Level rhs_sync_level=0, bool project_out_sync_levels=true, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol)
memberdef.name: compose

briefdescription.para: Composes two NFTs (lhs || rhs; read as "rhs after lhs").

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the composition of two NFTs, lhs and rhs, by aligning their synchronization levels. Transitions between two synchronization levels are ordered as follows: first the transitions of lhs, then the transitions of rhs followed by next synchronization level (if exists). By default, synchronization levels are projected out from the resulting NFT.

parameterdescription.para: First transducer to compose.
Second transducer to compose.
The synchronization level of the lhs.
The synchronization level of the rhs.
Whether we wont to project out the synchronization levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE.

simplesect.para: A new NFT after the composition.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, bool use_epsilon=false, StateRenaming *lhs_state_renaming=nullptr, StateRenaming *rhs_state_renaming=nullptr)
memberdef.name: concatenate

briefdescription.para: Concatenate two NFTs.

detaileddescription.para:
Supports epsilon symbols when use_epsilon is set to true.
lhs
First automaton to concatenate.
rhs
Second automaton to concatenate.
use_epsilon
Whether to concatenate over epsilon symbol.
lhs_state_renaming
Map mapping lhs states to result states.
rhs_state_renaming
Map mapping rhs states to result states.
Concatenated automaton.

briefdescription.para: Compute automaton accepting complement of aut.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton whose complement to compute.
Alphabet used for complementation.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &aut, const utils::OrdVector< Symbol > &symbols, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "classical" }, { "minimize", "false" }})
memberdef.name: complement

briefdescription.para: Compute automaton accepting complement of aut.

detaileddescription.para: This overloaded version complements over an already created ordered set of symbols instead of an alphabet. This is a more efficient solution in case you already have symbols precomputed or want to complement multiple automata over the same set of symbols: the function does not need to compute the ordered set of symbols from the alphabet again (and for each automaton).

parameterdescription.para: Automaton whose complement to compute.
Symbols to complement over.

briefdescription.para: Compute minimal deterministic automaton.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton whose minimal version to compute.

Optional parameters to control the minimization algorithm:
"algorithm": "brzozowski"

briefdescription.para: Determinize automaton.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton to determinize.
Map that maps sets of states of input automaton to states of determinized automaton.

briefdescription.para: Reduce the size of the automaton.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton to reduce.
Mapping of original states to reduced states.

Optional parameters to control the reduction algorithm:
"algorithm": "simulation".

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &smaller, const Nft &bigger, Run *cex, const Alphabet *alphabet=nullptr, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains" }})
memberdef.name: is_included

briefdescription.para: Checks inclusion of languages of two NFTs: smaller and bigger (smaller <= bigger).

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.
Counterexample for the inclusion.
Alphabet of both NFTs to compute with.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if smaller is included in bigger, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &smaller, const Nft &bigger, const Alphabet *const alphabet=nullptr, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains" }})
memberdef.name: is_included

briefdescription.para: Checks inclusion of languages of two NFTs: smaller and bigger (smaller <= bigger).

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.
Alphabet of both NFTs to compute with.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if smaller is included in bigger, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, const Alphabet *alphabet, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains"}})
memberdef.name: are_equivalent

briefdescription.para: Perform equivalence check of two NFTs: lhs and rhs.

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.
Alphabet of both NFTs to compute with.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if lhs and rhs are equivalent, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &lhs, const Nft &rhs, JumpMode JumpMode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains"}})
memberdef.name: are_equivalent

briefdescription.para: Perform equivalence check of two NFTs: lhs and rhs.

detaileddescription.para: The current implementation of Nft does not accept input alphabet. For this reason, an alphabet has to be created from all transitions each time an operation on alphabet is called. When calling this function, the alphabet has to be computed first.

Hence, this function is less efficient than its alternative taking already defined alphabet as its parameter. That way, alphabet has to be computed only once, as opposed to the current ad-hoc construction of the alphabet. The use of the alternative with defined alphabet should be preferred.

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if lhs and rhs are equivalent, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Inverts the levels of the given transducer aut.

detaileddescription.para: The function inverts the levels of the transducer, i.e., the level 0 becomes the last level, level 1 becomes the second last level, and so on.

parameterdescription.para: The transducer for inverting levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

simplesect.para: A new transducer with inverted levels.

briefdescription.para: Remove simple epsilon transitions.

detaileddescription.para: Simple epsilon transitions are the transitions of the form q0 -epsilon-> q1 -epsilon-> q2 -epsilon-> ... -epsilon-> qn where q0 and qn are level 0 states, the states in-between are states with level 1, 2, ..., num_of_levels and for each qi, for 0 < i < n, there is only 1 transition going to qi (the transition qi-1 -epsilon-> qi) and only 1 transition going from qi (the transition qi -epsilon -> qi+1). This means that if there was some state p0 going with epsilon to q1, these to epsilon transitions would not be removed.
Furthermore, this assumes that the NFT aut does not have jump transitions.
The resulting automaton has the same number of states as aut, just the transitions can change. It is recommended to run trim() after this function.

parameterdescription.para: NFT without jump transitions
symbol representing epsilon

simplesect.para: NFT whose language is same as aut but does not contain simple epsilon transitions

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &nft, const utils::OrdVector< Level > &levels_to_project, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol)
memberdef.name: project_out

briefdescription.para: Projects out specified levels levels_to_project in the given transducer nft.

parameterdescription.para: The transducer for projection.
A non-empty ordered vector of levels to be projected out from the transducer. It must contain only values that are greater than or equal to 0 and smaller than num_of_levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

briefdescription.para: Projects out specified level level_to_project in the given transducer nft.

parameterdescription.para: The transducer for projection.
A level that is going to be projected out from the transducer. It has to be greater than or equal to 0 and smaller than num_of_levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nft &nft, const utils::OrdVector< Level > &levels_to_project, JumpMode jump_mode=JumpMode::RepeatSymbol)
memberdef.name: project_to

briefdescription.para: Projects to specified levels levels_to_project in the given transducer nft.

parameterdescription.para: The transducer for projection.
A non-empty ordered vector of levels the transducer is going to be projected to. It must contain only values greater than or equal to 0 and smaller than num_of_levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

briefdescription.para: Projects to a specified level level_to_project in the given transducer nft.

parameterdescription.para: The transducer for projection.
A level the transducer is going to be projected to. It has to be greater than or equal to 0 and smaller than num_of_levels.

Specifies if the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

briefdescription.para: Inserts new levels, as specified by the mask new_levels_mask, into the given transducer nft.

detaileddescription.para: num_of_levels must be greater than 0. The vector new_levels_mask must be nonempty, its length must be greater than num_of_levels, and it must contain exactly num_of_levels occurrences of false.

parameterdescription.para: The original transducer.
A mask representing the old and new levels. The vector {1, 0, 1, 1, 0} indicates that one level is inserted before level 0 and two levels are inserted before level 1.

Specifies whether the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

briefdescription.para: Inserts a new level new_level into the given transducer nft.

parameterdescription.para: The original transducer.

Specifies the new level to be inserted into the transducer. If new_level is 0, then it is inserted before the 0-th level. If new_level is less than num_of_levels, then it is inserted before the level new_level-1. If new_level is greater than or equal to num_of_levels, then all levels from num_of_levels through new_level are appended after the last level.

Specifies whether the symbol on a jump transition (a transition with a length greater than 1) is interpreted as a sequence repeating the same symbol or as a single instance of the symbol followed by a sequence of DONT_CARE symbols.

briefdescription.para: Encodes a vector of strings (each corresponding to one symbol) into a Word instance.
